meta.title: Shah Fahad · Fullstack Engineer
meta.description: Fullstack engineer in Frankfurt with five years building React and Node applications in production, now studying Web and Data Science. Open to fullstack and data roles.

hero.lead: Five years building React and Node applications in production, most recently as a Senior Software Engineer on a 17 person team. I'm now doing an M.Sc. in Web and Data Science at the University of Koblenz, moving toward work that sits between the two.

metrics.label: Lighthouse improvement on a live product

paragraphs: I'm a fullstack engineer. Most of my five years has been on the frontend side of that, React, TypeScript, Next.js, but I've designed the REST and WebSocket APIs behind those interfaces, shaped the database schemas under them, and owned the CI/CD that shipped the whole thing. The work I like most is where the two sides meet. Making a real time pipeline actually feel real time. Cutting a Lighthouse score from 35 to 80 without breaking what the page was there to do in the first place.

I spent just over four years at Creative Morph (RemoteBase), an engineering company building for external clients, joining in 2022 as a Software Engineer I and leaving in June 2026 as a Senior Software Engineer I. Before that I built dashboards and a payment gateway at AlgoRack in Karachi. Most of it was product engineering for client teams, which means learning an unfamiliar codebase quickly, working inside constraints I didn't set, and shipping anyway. The role was fully remote.

I relocated to Germany in 2025 to start an M.Sc. in Web and Data Science at the University of Koblenz. That's the direction I'm going. I've integrated the Claude and OpenAI APIs into production products, and I'm now learning the statistics and machine learning underneath properly instead of treating the model as a box that returns text. I live in Frankfurt and I'm looking for work here.

education.detail: Mohammad Ali Jinnah University, Karachi · 2017 to 2021

experience.lead: Five years of product engineering, most of it building for client teams under constraints I didn't set. Every number below is something I measured, not an estimate.

companies.name: Creative Morph (RemoteBase)
companies.description: Engineering company building products for external clients.

roles.title: Senior Software Engineer I
bullets: Built real time monitoring pipelines with WebSockets and React Query, cutting insight delivery from 15 minutes to 2 minutes.
Designed REST and WebSocket APIs that reduced data sync latency by 60%.
Raised Lighthouse scores from 35 to 80 through memoization, code splitting, and bundle optimization.
Integrated the Claude and OpenAI APIs, enabling 5 new GenAI use cases and cutting routine task time by 30%.
Led delivery within a 17 engineer cross functional team, reviewing 5 to 10 pull requests weekly.

roles.title: Software Engineer II
bullets: Built a shared component library of 15+ reusable components, adopted by 4 engineers across 3 production services and cutting duplicate code by 40%.
Migrated state management, reducing bundle size by 18%.
Redesigned CI/CD with Docker, cutting build time by 35% and infrastructure costs by 20%.

roles.title: Software Engineer I
bullets: Shipped a product MVP as sole engineer in 6 weeks, covering frontend, API layer, and database schema.
Built 4 REST API integrations serving 200+ enterprise users monthly.

roles.title: Software Developer
bullets: Built dashboards integrating 6 REST API sources for 3 enterprise clients.
Built a payment gateway processing 10,000+ monthly transactions and lifting sales conversion by 15%.

contact.lead: The fastest way to reach me is email. I'm open to fullstack and data roles in Germany, full time or working student, in English-speaking or bilingual teams.

skills.lead: The stack I've shipped with, and the tools I'm picking up through my M.Sc. I've split them honestly, what I've used in production sits apart from what I'm still learning.
